target.name: Twelve

methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median

private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Twelve is a Series C climate and energy company that has raised $472M+ to date. Valuation analysis triangulates across a peer set of three same-sector, same-stage private companies: KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M). The broader comparable universe includes eight private frontier-tech peers in climate and energy, with one relevant public reference point.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses total capital raised ($472M) as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $802M. Peer-median analysis across the three identified comparables produces a $1.0B implied value, with the peer set ranging from $700M to $2.0B. The peer cohort's 3x spread reflects variance in business model maturity and sector subsegment.

Triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $682M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median point of $901M. The midpoint sits between the stage-progression result and peer median, weighted toward the lower quartile of observed peer valuations. Key uncertainties include the early-stage nature of climate tech development timelines, the nascency of several comparable business models, and limited public market data in the sector. Resolution of technology risk and demonstration of unit economics represent primary value inflection points.
